signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: GBP/AUD exhibits a critical G8 bilateral divergence with a 1.479% spread between the week's leader and laggard. Despite a -60bp carry deficit for GBP vs AUD (BoE 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), GBP outperformance dominates as AUD faces broad-based selling. The spread exceeds the 1.20% threshold for a strong theme, signaling a structural rotation out of high-beta AUD into GBP. GBP/AUD at 1.8835.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Geopolitical Risk + Hawkish Fed Bias
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3814 as US-Iran geopolitical uncertainty and hawkish commentary from Fed Governor Waller drive USD demand. Waller's warning on inflation expectations supports the +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%). While RBC expects Canadian GDP support, the 125bp OIS spread (SOFR 3.50% vs CORRA 2.25%) and geopolitical safe-haven flows favor USD upside. Watch for a sustained break above 1.3850 to confirm trend extension.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (specs fading the RBNZ) vs a hawkish market bias for the 27 May meeting. With retail sentiment at 71% long, a contrarian risk exists if the RBNZ fails to deliver a hawkish surprise, potentially triggering a long-unwind toward 0.5800. However, if the RBNZ signals a hike, the crowded spec short faces a squeeze. NZD/USD at 0.5859.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7845 shows an extreme +5.8% Vol Risk Premium (IV 12.7% vs HV30 6.9%), signaling the options market is pricing in significant tail risk compared to recent realized movement. Despite a massive +375bp carry advantage for USD (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%), the neutral 25d RR (-0.01) suggests the market is not yet paying for upside protection, focusing instead on hedging volatility. Carry remains the primary driver unless VIX exceeds 25.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.18 remains supported by the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry spread. 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Options market shows a moderate put skew (25d RR at -1.22), indicating institutional hedging against a JPY rally/intervention despite the carry-driven bid. Watch Tokyo CPI for BoJ hike confirmation.
